summary:
Phosphoglucomutase catalyzes the interconversion of glucose-1-phosphate to glucose-6-phosphate in both glycogenesis and glycogenolysis. This shared enzymatic activity links glycolytic and gluconeogenic flux through a central metabolic node. 

Note: The summary should not include any information about answer choices or boxes unless explicitly required by the prompt.

The correct enzyme common to both glycogenesis and glycogenolysis is phosphoglucomutase. It converts glucose-1-phosphate to glucose-6-phosphate during both synthesis and breakdown of glycogen.  

(Note: Summary strictly adhered to only essential clinical facts; no extraneous details included.)  
Final Answer:
